cannot_score_reason: This video is primarily instructional/educational content showing FLS equipment setup, components, and trainer assembly rather than a performance of a specific FLS task.

content_observed.frames_1_3: Person speaking to camera in home setting — appears to be introducing or discussing FLS training content
content_observed.frames_4_6: Close-up views of FLS trainer box with camera mount and optical tracking system
content_observed.frames_7_9: Individual laparoscopic instruments displayed: graspers, Maryland dissector, needle driver with price labels ($30, $40, $120)
content_observed.frames_10_12: Overhead views of trainer with blue peg transfer apparatus, white pegs visible
content_observed.frames_13_15: Hand performing peg transfer task movements — transferring objects on pegs
content_observed.frames_16_18: Display of 6 blue rubber or foam peg objects arranged in circular tray; hand manipulating trainer setup
content_observed.frames_19_23: User interface showing laparoscopic trainer video feed on monitor; trainee hands visible working at trainer table

video_components_identified: Peg transfer apparatus with blue pegs
classification_rationale: This video documents FLS equipment, setup procedures, and general training overview rather than scoring a single trainee performance on a timed task. Narration by an instructor, equipment demonstrations, and setup assembly are evident. No continuous timed performance of a single task (e.g., complete peg transfer in 300s, pattern cut in 300s) is shown from start to finish with measurable completion time.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper laparoscopic instrument handling and grip
FLS trainer box setup and calibration
Camera positioning for task video capture
teaching_content.verbal_cues: Implied from instructor-led presentation; specific narration not clearly legible from frames
common_errors_addressed: Correct instrument selection for specific tasks
Proper positioning of trainer box and camera

recommendation: This video is suitable for educational orientation to FLS training but does NOT contain a scoreable performance. To score a task, provide a video showing: (1) a specific task ID (task 1–5), (2) continuous capture of task execution from start to completion or timeout, and (3) visible timestamps or phase markers for penalty calculation.
